site.title: Amur Tiger SSP Resources
site.description: For people working on or following Amur tiger conservation across AZA institutions.

pages.title: Home
pages.intro: A door for the Amur tiger conservation community.
body: Amur tigers are the largest wild cats on earth. There are fewer than 500 left in the wild. The Species Survival Plan coordinates breeding, care, and conservation efforts across accredited zoos and aquariums in North America. This site is for people already working in that space — keepers, curators, researchers, volunteers, and the people who follow this work closely enough to care about the details. It does not replace the SSP listserv, your institution's internal resources, or the relationships you already have. It points toward them.

pages.title: Where People Should Go
body: These places are chosen by the site creator. FYN does not maintain a directory or infer relationships between communities.

links.label: AZA Species Survival Plan Program
links.description: The SSP program coordinates population management and conservation efforts across AZA-accredited institutions.

links.label: Wildlife Conservation Society — Amur Tiger Program
links.description: Field conservation work in the Russian Far East, including anti-poaching efforts and habitat protection.

links.label: Panthera — Tigers Forever
links.description: Science-based tiger conservation across range countries, with specific Amur tiger work in Russia.

links.label: AZA Animal Care Manuals
links.description: Husbandry guidelines developed by SSP working groups. The felid manual covers tiger care standards.

links.label: ZIMS — Species360
links.description: The zoological information management system used for population records and studbook data.
